name: Sweet and Sour Chicken Balls
recipeIngredient: 1 can Pineapple chunks in own juice (14 ounces)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a medium bowl, mix together the egg, garlic, salt, and pepper. Add the ground chicken and 2 tablespoons of the flour. Sprinkle the remaining flour on a sheet of waxed paper.
2. Drop the chicken mixture by heaping teaspoons onto the floured waxed paper and roll in the flour to form balls.
3. Heat the canola oil in a nonstick frypan over high heat. Add the chicken balls and cook, turning occasionally until brown, about 6 minutes. Remove the chicken balls and drain on paper towels.
4. Reserve 1 tablespoon of oil in the frypan. Drain the pineapple juice into a measuring cup; add water to make 1 cup liquid. Dissolve the bouillon cube in the pineapple juice.
5. Add the green pepper slices to the pan and stir until heated through, about 2 minutes.
6. In a small bowl, combine the cornstarch, sugar, and ginger. Stir in the soy sauce and vinegar until smooth.
7. Pour the cornstarch mixture into the pan with the pineapple juice and green peppers. Stir constantly and cook until clear and thickened, about 5 minutes.
8. Arrange the chicken balls over the sauce and serve with hot cooked rice and crisp Chinese noodles.
